notes: 不必抄題，作答時請將試題題號及答案依照順序寫在申論試卷上，於本試題上作答者，不予計分。
請以藍、黑色鋼筆或原子筆在申論試卷上作答。
本試題為單一選擇題，請選出一個正確或最適當答案。
共40題，每題1.25分，須用2B鉛筆在試卡上依題號清楚劃記，於本試題或申論試卷上作答者，不予計分。

questions.stem:
英譯中(20 分)
Taiwan has the technological know-how to make a wide range of drones
from cheap, small suicide-drones like the ones used in Ukraine to more
complex reconnaissance drones that are customised to take off from
moving warships and withstand strong winds in the Taiwan Strait. Its main
problem is not technology, but scale. China controls 80% of the global
drone market, which means it can produce drones very cheaply. The non-
red drones Coretronic makes cost about 25% more than their equivalent in
China. Taiwan’s electronics manufacturers need more orders from abroad
so that they can scale up.
* Coretronic(中強光電)

英文作文(30 分)
Unsafe drivers can be of any age, particularly when drinking is involved.
While younger drivers may be inexperienced or more likely to be
distracted or reckless, older drivers often overestimate their driving
abilities. That may be one reason many unsafe older drivers continue to
drive despite failing driving skills. Balancing public safety with seniors’
independence is a complex issue. For many older adults, driving is
essential for staying active, running errands, attending social events,
visiting loved ones, and maintaining control over their daily routines.
In your opinion, when should seniors stop driving, and who should make that
decision?
Write a short essay of approximately 250 words explaining your
perspective. Support your argument with clear reasoning, and if possible,
real-life examples.

questions.passage:
請依下文回答第21題至第25題
Peopletellstories forvarious reasons. Somestoriesaretoldtoentertain, while others aim to pass on wisdom, knowledge, and morals to future generations. For young children, stories play a crucial role in theirlives. Notonlydochildrenlearnabouttheworldaroundthemthroughstoriesbut 21 , theybuild up a foundation for future learning as well. Researchhasshownthatthereisastrongassociationbetween children’s success in school and their experiences with storybooks and storytelling in their mother 22 from an early age. Children who 23 well on literacy tests tend to have stories read or told to them before starting their formal education. In regard to the significance of stories in children's literacy development, studies suggest that most of the new vocabulary children 24 in their school years are acquired through stories they read or listen to. Thevalueofstories, nevertheless, shouldnot belimitedto language acquisition; stories can also bring gains in personal, emotional and learning development in children 25 thoseinlanguage.

questions.passage:
請依下文回答第26題至第30題
Because of their valuable cargo and minimal defenses, merchant ships make especially attractive targets for maritime pirates. Inresponse, some private companies offer security services to 26 merchant ships’ defensive capacities. Vessels can hire armed security personnel to accompany them on voyages. Or, an escort vessel can be hired to 27 dangerous shipping lanes. Because of the high risk and expense 28 thelatterservice, it’s an optionthat wouldlikelyonlybe usedbyships carryingrareor most valuable commodities. In fact, many shipping firms choose to forego any additional security 29 , opting instead to simply pay the ransom should one of their vessels be hijacked. Unfortunately, 30 say, doingso emboldens hijackers and encourages piracy.

questions.stem: Whichofthefollowingsentences best expresses themainideaofthe first paragraph?

options.A: Microbeads are contaminating waterways ina harmful manner.
options.B: Microbeads are appearing in the tap water people use everyday.
options.C: Microbeads are a clever design to cleanse the pores and remove surface stains from teeth.
options.D: Microbeads are clogging up the bathroom drains and wastewater treatment systems.

questions.passage:
請依下文回答第31題至第35題
If you are a person who cares about hygiene, you may have noticed little beads in exfoliating facial scrubs and toothpaste. What you may not know is that these microbeads are actually balls of plastic that are smaller than five millimeters in diameter. They were designed to be easily flushed down the drain. However, because water treatment plants were not built to filter them out, micro beads are polluting rivers, oceans, andlakes inaserious yet not immediately obvious way. According to a study that was published in the scientific journal Environmental Science and Technology in 2015, eight trillion microbeads were released into aquatic habitats daily in the US alone. Since then, this estimate has continued to rise, contributing to the ever-growing global microplastic problem. Microbeads aren’t biodegradable, which is bad news for both marine life and humans. The micro beads function as sponges and end up attracting dangerous chemicals like pesticides. Someanimals, suchasfish, cannot distinguish the beads from good and windup eating them. Inturn, thesesameanimals are then eaten by humans. Asaresult, these beads radically impact human health as they move from being chemicals in water to being part of the food chain. What’s worse is that because of their size, theyareincredibly difficult to cleanup. Usingthemhasputusonasurefirepathtoharmingtheplanet’s wildlife and water quality. “Beat the Microbead” is an international campaign initiated in 2012bythe Plastic Soup Foundation to raise the general public’s awareness about the environmental impact of microbeads. It has pressured cosmetic companies to phase out the use of beads. It has even released an app that allows people to scan the barcodes of products to see whether they contain microbeads. Identifying the complex names in an ingredients list can be difficult for consumers. However, the best solution at hand is to ban the use of micro beads outright and figure out away to clean our oceans. Anotherwayistoadoptnaturalalternatives that are non-toxic and biodegradable to replace microbeads. Researchers of the study concluded, “The probabilityofrisk from microbe ad pollution is high, while the solution to this problem is simple.”

questions.stem: Whichofthefollowingsentences best expresses the main idea of the second paragraph?

options.A: Microbeads are, infact, flammable and can easily catch fire in the presence of a heat source.
options.D: Chemicalsareattractedtomicrobeads, andwhencombined, they become toxic substances that appear asfoodtofish.

questions.stem: What is thewriter’s purposeinthefirst paragraph?

options.A: Todescribethe financial advantages ofusing AIagents over humanemployees
options.B: Toexplainthetechnical capabilities of AIagents like Avainsales-relatedtasks
options.D: Toillustratethegrowingconcernabout AI’s potential to replace human workers in the job market

questions.passage:
請依下文回答第36題至第40題
The impact of artificial intelligence on employment is a prominent topic, as highlighted by a sign reading “Stop Hiring Humans” at an AI conference, reflecting the unease surrounding new AI models. Artisan, a startup, exemplifies this shift byoffering AI agents like Ava, virtual sales representatives that can identify leads, contact customers, write emails, and schedule appointments, costing 96 percent less than human equivalents. This development is part of the generative AI trend that began with Chat GPT in 2022, where AI increasinglymakes decisions traditionally made by humans. While Artisan, one of the key players inthis field, is upfront about the possibility of AI replacing certain jobs, many other generative AI companies takeamorecautious stance. Thesecompanies arguethat ratherthan replacingworkers, AIwill primarily serve as a tool to enhance human capabilities, allowing individuals to perform their jobs more efficiently andcreatively. Predictionsregarding AI's impact vary significantly. Goldman Sachsestimates300millionpotentialjob lossesglobally. AMetrigyreportindicatedthat89percentofsurveyedfirmsreducedcustomerrelationsstaff duetogenerative AI.Conversely, the World Economic Forumfoundthat70percentofmajorcompaniesplan to hire workers with AI-related skills. This is seen by some as a “natural evolution,” with new sectors and jobs being created while others are lost, similar to the impact of the car. USDepartmentof Labordatashows a decline in secretarial roles and arise in computer scientists, coinciding with the rise of office computing. Given the potential for displacement, some advise discretion when promoting AIthat replaces parts ofateam, with some clients preferring to keep their AIusageconfidential. Analystspredictjoblossesforroles likeprogrammers, call center operators, translators, andtravelagents. However, some startup s argue that their AIfocusesonrevenuegrowth, which can lead to more human jobs. Othersnotethatwhilecompaniesmaynot wanttolayoffstaff, they are looking to grow without additional hiring. Transparencyabout AI'simplications is crucial to avoid public backlash, as the development of AIisconsideredbysometobe“inevitable.”

questions.stem: What is thewriter’s purpose in the second paragraph?

options.B: To provide contrasting viewpoints on whether AI will take over jobs or serve as a tool to assist workers
options.C: Toarguethat AIwill inevitably replace jobs inindustries likesales and customer service
options.D: To provide a historical overview of how generative AI evolved, beginning with the launch of Chat GPT

options.A: Predictions about AI’s impact onjobs areconsistent across different sources.

Analystspredictjoblossesforroles likeprogrammers, call center operators, translators, andtravelagents. However, some startup s argue that their AIfocusesonrevenuegrowth, which can lead to more human jobs. Othersnotethatwhilecompaniesmaynot wanttolayoffstaff, they are looking to grow without additional hiring. Transparencyabout AI'simplications is crucial to avoid public backlash, as the development of AIisconsideredbysometobe“inevitable.”

questions.stem: What is the main idea of the fourth paragraph?

options.A: While AImight lead to job reductions, it could also create new roles, and organizations must be clear about its impact to manage public perception.
options.B: The growing use of AI technology is inevitable and should be embraced without concern for potential joblosses.
options.D: The use of AIin the workplace is primarily beneficial for businesses lookingto reduce staff and expenses.

請依下文回答第36題至第40題
The impact of artificial intelligence on employment is a prominent topic, as highlighted by a sign reading “Stop Hiring Humans” at an AI conference, reflecting the unease surrounding new AI models. Artisan, a startup, exemplifies this shift byoffering AI agents like Ava, virtual sales representatives that can identify leads, contact customers, write emails, and schedule appointments, costing 96 percent less than human equivalents. This development is part of the generative AI trend that began with Chat GPT in 2022, where AI increasinglymakes decisions traditionally made by humans. While Artisan, one of the key players inthis field, is upfront about the possibility of AI replacing certain jobs, many other generative AI companies takeamorecautious stance. Thesecompanies arguethat ratherthan replacingworkers, AIwill primarily serve as a tool to enhance human capabilities, allowing individuals to perform their jobs more efficiently andcreatively. Predictionsregarding AI's impact vary significantly. Goldman Sachsestimates300millionpotentialjob lossesglobally. AMetrigyreportindicatedthat89percentofsurveyedfirmsreducedcustomerrelationsstaff duetogenerative AI.Conversely, the World Economic Forumfoundthat70percentofmajorcompaniesplan to hire workers with AI-related skills. This is seen by some as a “natural evolution,” with new sectors and jobs being created while others are lost, similar to the impact of the car. USDepartmentof Labordatashows a decline in secretarial roles and arise in computer scientists, coinciding with the rise of office computing. Given the potential for displacement, some advise discretion when promoting AIthat replaces parts ofateam, with some clients preferring to keep their AIusageconfidential. Analystspredictjoblossesforroles likeprogrammers, call center operators, translators, andtravelagents. However, some startup s argue that their AIfocusesonrevenuegrowth, which can lead to more human jobs. Othersnotethatwhilecompaniesmaynot wanttolayoffstaff, they are looking to grow without additional hiring. Transparencyabout AI'simplications is crucial to avoid public backlash, as the development of AIisconsideredbysometobe“inevitable.”
